chunk_summary: This section outlines tele-safe supplementation policies for anemia and micronutrient deficiencies, emphasizing dosing, monitoring, and stop rules under tele-nutrition frameworks aligned with Indian health guidelines.
learning_objectives: Understand tele-nutrition policies for safe supplementation of anemia and micronutrient deficiencies.
Learn therapeutic dosing guidelines for iron, vitamin D, and vitamin A in children based on Indian health protocols.
Recognize monitoring and stop rules for tele-prescribed supplements to prevent toxicity or misuse.
key_facts: Therapeutic iron dosing for mild/moderate anemia in children: 3 mg/kg/day elemental iron for 2 months with 14-day follow-ups (MoHFW guidance).
Vitamin D prophylaxis in infancy: 400 IU/day from birth to 1 year (IAP guidance).
National vitamin A prophylaxis: 9 doses from 9 months to 5 years, including 100,000 IU at 9 months and 200,000 IU annually from 1–5 years (NHM guidelines).
Deworming protocol: Albendazole 200 mg for ages 1–2 years and 400 mg for ages 2–19 years, avoiding deworming during acute illness (NHM guidance).
Preterm/low birth weight infants: Enteral iron dosing commonly 2–3 mg/kg/day per WHO evidence summaries.
reference_quote: Prescribing decisions remain an RMP responsibility, and medication categories are restricted by consult type and modality. (Telemedicine Practice Guidelines, MoHFW)
document_key_topics: Tele-nutrition policies for anemia and micronutrient deficiencies
Safe supplementation dosing and monitoring protocols
Indian health guidelines for iron, vitamin D, and vitamin A
Integration of deworming into anemia and growth pathways
